questions.q: Let <math><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>c</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> be three vectors such that <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>∙</mo><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>c</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></math> and the angle between <math><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> and <math><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>c</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is <math><mfrac><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>.</mo></math> If <math><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></math> is perpendicular to the vector <math><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>c</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>b</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>×</mo><mover accent="true"><mrow><mi>c</mi></mrow><mo>→</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to ____________.
questions.solution:
b→.c→=10⇒b→c→cos⁡π3=10⇒5.c→.12=10

⇒c→=4

Also, a→.b→×c→=0

a→×b→×c→=a→b→×c→sin⁡π2

⇒3×b→c→sin⁡π3×1

⇒3×5×4×32=30

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>56</mn><mn>17</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>43</mn><mn>17</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>111</mn><mn>17</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> be the vertices of the rhombus <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mi>R</mi><mi>Q</mi><mi>S</mi></math>. If the direction ratios of the diagonal <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi><mi>S</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math>, where both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and $\beta$ are integers of minimum absolute values, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,

 P-2,-1,1 and Q5617,4317,11117 be the vertices of the rhombus PRQS. If the direction ratios of the diagonal RS are α,-1,β, where both α and β are integers of minimum absolute values, then α2+β2 is equal to

Now RS=α,-1,β

So, direction ratio of PQ≡5617+2,4317+1,11117-1

≡9017,6017,9417

Now we know that diagonal of rhombus are perpendicular,

So, 9017α+6017-1+9417β=0

⇒90α+94β=60

⇒β=60-90α94

⇒β=302-3α94

⇒β=-303α-294

⇒β=-15473α-2

⇒β-15=3α-247

⇒β=-15,α=-15 as α & β are integer

So, α2+β2=225+225=450
